title: "Add to Calendar" (ICS) and Daily Report

posts.author: MelissaC1 (Melissa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!
I’m new to Monday and this is my first question! I have a two-part question.

I’m creating an event form that I will send out to customers via automation. In the confirmation email, I want to include “Add to Calendar” link that will add the event to customer’s calendar. I researched and saw an addon “AddEvent” but could not find it in the market place.

I see that every time a form is submitted, an email is sent. I would like to instead get an email at the end of the day with a summary of the forms submitted.

All suggestions are welcomed!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @MelissaC1

You can build a link to create an event by inserting the info into a URL and adding it to your Email template.

Past this example link into ChatGPT, give it the Title, description, date, start and end time, and ask it to reformatt it.

https://calendar.google.com/calendar/render?action=TEMPLATE&text=Customer%20Onboarding%20Webinar&details=Learn%20how%20to%20get%20started%20quickly.%20Includes%20live%20Q%26A.&location=Online%20(Zoom)&dates=20250910T160000Z/20250910T170000Z

posts.author: MelissaC1 (Melissa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Desiree,
I’m still getting to know Monday, been working on integration with Jira. But now switching back to this project. I have “monday work management Enterprise”

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
For the second part of the question - I’d recommend using a dashboardwith with a table that’s filtered for ‘today’. Then do a schedule PDF export.

I need a little more detail on how to achieve this. I need to have this for outlook and apple calendars.
